claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 12 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 12 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 12 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's start the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm actually asking about two of my children - Alex who is 12 and Blake who is 8. So they're both under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great that you're checking eligibility for both Alex (12) and Blake (8). Since both children are under 16, they both automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Let me continue the assessment for both children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?

Given their ages (12 and 8), I would expect they're not working, but I just need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all - they're still in primary and secondary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I expected given their ages. Since neither Alex nor Blake are working, they both pass this check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are Alex and Blake **not** receiving any benefits in their own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

Again, given their ages and that they're in school, this is likely the case - but I need to confirm that neither child is receiving these benefits themselves.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - neither of them receive any benefits in their own names.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you for confirming. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are Alex and Blake currently **not** in an apprenticeship?

Since you mentioned they're in primary and secondary school, I assume neither of them is doing an apprenticeship - but I just need to confirm this.

*(For reference: most apprenticeships disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except for Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in any apprenticeships - they're just doing regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Since both children are in regular school and not in any apprenticeships, they pass this check too. Now I need to ask some questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to check your residency status.**

Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You are a resident in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can support yourself financially (without claiming certain benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are a student with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as a family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You are living abroad as a Crown servant

Which of these applies to you? (Most commonly, people simply normally live in the UK.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Since you normally live in the UK, you meet the residency requirements. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Are you responsible for Alex and Blake? You meet this requirement if **any** of the following apply:

1. **Living with the children** - Alex and Blake live with you
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - You pay at least the equivalent of Child Benefit towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - You make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, since Alex and Blake both live with you, you clearly have responsibility for them. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?

- **No** - No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child
- **Yes, but we've agreed** - Someone else was claiming, but you've agreed that you should now receive it instead
- **Yes, and it's disputed** - Someone else is claiming and there's a disagreement about who should receive it

Which applies to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that keeps things straightforward. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question:**

Where are Alex and Blake currently living? I need to know if any special circumstances apply:

- **Normal living** - They live at home with you (or in a normal living arrangement)
- **In hospital** - Either child is currently staying in hospital
- **In care** - Either child is currently in local authority care
- **Fostered** - Either child is currently being fostered

Which applies to your children?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're just living normally at home with me - nothing special like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Final question:**

Is it the case that the local council is **not** paying towards Alex's or Blake's accommodation or maintenance?

In other words, the council isn't providing financial support specifically for the children's living costs or upkeep. (This would typically only apply if social services were involved.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That completes the assessment. Since the council is not paying towards either child, both Alex and Blake meet all the eligibility criteria.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great news! I've completed the assessment for both children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Good News - You're Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Both Alex and Blake qualify for Child Benefit.**

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| Alex | 12 | ✅ Yes |
| Blake | 8 | ✅ Yes |

### Next Steps to Claim

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex and Blake

### Important Information

- **Payments** are made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)
- **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: Be aware that if you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay some or all of the benefit back through a tax charge. You can still claim, but you may choose to opt out of receiving payments to avoid the charge.

### Useful Links

- Apply for Child Benefit: [gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim)

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 12) is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They do not receive benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK, 6) The claimant has responsibility for Alex (lives with them), 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, 8) Alex lives at home in a normal living arrangement, and 9) The local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They do not receive benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK, 6) The claimant has responsibility for Blake (lives with them), 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, 8) Blake lives at home in a normal living arrangement, and 9) The local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Both Alex (12) and Blake (8) are eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in Manchester, UK, with both children at home. Neither child is working, receiving benefits, or in an apprenticeship. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility criteria are met for both children.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 12) is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They do not receive benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK, 6) The claimant has responsibility for Alex (lives with them), 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, 8) Alex lives at home in a normal living arrangement, and 9) The local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 8) is eligible for Child Benefit because: 1) They are under 16 years old, 2) They are not working, 3) They do not receive benefits in their own right, 4) They are not in a disqualifying apprenticeship, 5) The claimant lives in the UK, 6) The claimant has responsibility for Blake (lives with them), 7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, 8) Blake lives at home in a normal living arrangement, and 9) The local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: Both Alex (12) and Blake (8) are eligible for Child Benefit. The claimant lives in Manchester, UK, with both children at home. Neither child is working, receiving benefits, or in an apprenticeship. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility criteria are met for both children.
